SUMMARY_EVIDENCE: Policy requires all participating modules to be canonical; the only enforcement hook is assertModulesCanonical(moduleNames), which validates a caller-supplied list. There is no automatic module-participation model, no mandatory modules_used across Switching/analysis, and the sole registry consumer is analyze_phi_kappa_canonical_space.m (assertModulesCanonical({'Switching'})). Mis-declaration or omission cannot be detected; canonical run_switching_canonical.m does not invoke the assert.

module_canonical_status.csv is read only where assertModulesCanonical runs; Switching/analysis/run_switching_canonical.m (registry-listed canonical entrypoint per docs/repo_execution_rules.md) does not read it. The table is manually maintained (three MODULE rows), can drift from code reality, and is not complete for hypothetical future modules unless extended.

Repository-wide grep: assertModulesCanonical appears only in Switching/utils/assertModulesCanonical.m and Switching/analysis/analyze_phi_kappa_canonical_space.m. Missing: canonical runner, failure allocation paths, other analysis scripts, tools/load_observables.m (uses run-level status not module_canonical_status), and any direct or uninstrumented MATLAB runs.

tables/module_enforcement_status.csv sets CROSS_MODULE_PROTECTION_ACTIVE=YES while runtime cross-module canonical checks are effectively opt-in and non-coextensive with canonical Switching execution; operators may infer protection that does not apply to the primary entrypoint.
See MINIMAL_ISOLATION_REQUIREMENT in isolation_feasibility_status.csv for the condition needed for CANONICAL_SYSTEM_ISOLATED=YES.
